items.title: John Ternus to become Apple CEO
items.author: schappim
items.summary: John Ternus will become Apple's CEO on September 1, succeeding Tim Cook, who is transitioning to Executive Chairman. Ternus has been with Apple since 2001 and has been instrumental in product development. This marks the first leadership change in 15 years.

items.title: Anthropic says OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage is allowed again
items.author: jmsflknr
items.summary: Anthropic has reversed its policy and now allows OpenClaw-style Claude CLI usage again. Previously, third-party access was restricted, but this restriction has been lifted. Users can now use OpenClaw with their Claude accounts without extra charges.

items.title: Show HN: Mediator.ai – Using Nash bargaining and LLMs to systematize fairness
items.author: sanity
items.text:
Eight years ago, my then-fiancée and I decided to get a prenup, so we hired a local mediator. The meetings were useful, but I felt there was no systematic process to produce a final agreement. So I started to think about this problem, and after a bit of research, I discovered the Nash bargaining solution.

Yet if John Nash had solved negotiation in the 1950s, why did it seem like nobody was using it today? The issue was that Nash's solution required that each party to the negotiation provi
items.summary: Mediator.ai uses Nash bargaining and LLMs to systemize fairness in negotiations, offering automated, impartial solutions for disputes. It leverages AI to draft and evaluate agreements, ensuring balanced outcomes. The platform has been applied in various cooperative scenarios, including business partnerships and personal conflicts.

items.title: The Beauty of Bonsai Styles
items.author: lagniappe
items.summary: Bonsai styles include formal upright, cascade, broom, clump, and literati. Each style mimics natural tree forms. The most common is the formal upright style.

items.title: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview: Smarter, Sharper, Still Evolving
items.author: mfiguiere
items.summary: Qwen3.6-Max-Preview is a proprietary model with enhanced world knowledge and agentic coding. It follows Qwen3.6-Plus and is available via Alibaba Cloud Model Studio API. Qwen3.6-35B-A3B, a 35B parameter model, was also open-sourced.

items.title: How a subsea cable is repaired
items.author: slicktux
items.summary: To repair a subsea cable, a cable ship locates the break, uses a grapnel to snare the damaged section, raises it to the surface, and cuts out the damaged part before splicing in a new section.

items.title: A Roblox cheat and one AI tool brought down Vercel's platform
items.author: bishwasbh
items.summary: Vercel's platform was compromised by a Roblox cheat and a compromised AI tool, exposing internal systems and non-sensitive data. The breach led to scrutiny of API keys used by crypto developers. Vercel acknowledged the breach and advised affected projects to secure their credentials.

items.title: Ternary Bonsai: Top Intelligence at 1.58 Bits
items.author: nnx
items.summary: Ternary Bonsai is a family of 1.58-bit language models that use ternary weights, achieving high accuracy while significantly reducing memory usage compared to standard models. They outperform most 1-bit models and run faster on various devices.

items.title: Air Is Full of DNA
items.author: howrude
items.summary: Airborne DNA helps scientists monitor ecosystem health, detect invasive species, and identify humans, contributing to environmental and public health studies. This genetic material is collected from the air and analyzed for these purposes.

items.title: Japan's cherry blossom database, 1,200 years old, has a new keeper
items.author: caycep
items.summary: Genki Katata, an environmental biophysicist, has taken over the 1,200-year-old cherry blossom database from the late Yasuyuki Aono. This record tracks the blooming dates of cherry blossoms in Kyoto. Katata will continue the important work of monitoring climate impacts on this tradition.

items.title: Soul Player C64 – A real transformer running on a 1 MHz Commodore 64
items.author: adunk
items.summary: Soul Player C64 is a project that runs a 25k-parameter transformer on a 1 MHz Commodore 64. It's a remarkable feat of computing. The project has been shared on Hacker News and Reddit.

items.title: All phones sold in the EU to have replaceable batteries from 2027
items.author: ramonga
items.summary: Starting in 2027, all phones sold in the EU will have replaceable batteries and USB-C chargers. This regulation aims to reduce electronic waste and promote repairability. The rules also require manufacturers to provide spare parts for at least 7 years.

items.title: Year of the IPv6 Overlay Network
items.author: stock_toaster
items.summary: The first IPv6 production network was established in 1999. The year 2026 marks the 25th anniversary of this milestone. IPv6 overlay networks have been discussed since at least 2024.

items.title: OpenAI ad partner now selling ChatGPT ad placements based on “prompt relevance”
items.author: jlark77777
items.summary: OpenAI has started selling ads in its AI chatbot, charging per view rather than per click, and has partnered with Criteo and Smartly for ad placements and interactive ads.

items.title: Zero-Copy Pages in Rust: Or How I Learned to Stop Worrying and Love Lifetimes
items.author: ingve
items.summary: Rust's zero-copy pages allow efficient data transfer without copying, leveraging lifetimes for memory safety. This technique enhances performance in systems programming. The concept is gaining recognition in the Rust community.

items.title: Atlassian enables default data collection to train AI
items.author: kevcampb
items.summary: Atlassian will start collecting data from Jira and Confluence by default on August 17, 2026, to train its AI models. Free and Standard customers cannot opt out of metadata collection. Data retention is up to seven years.

items.title: Deezer says 44% of songs uploaded to its platform daily are AI-generated
items.author: FiddlerClamp
items.summary: Deezer reports that 44% of daily uploads are AI-generated, with nearly 75,000 synthetic tracks per day, though AI-generated music accounts for only 1-3% of total streams. Deezer claims to be the first to independently detect and tag AI-generated music.

items.title: OpenClaw isn't fooling me. I remember MS-DOS
items.author: feigewalnuss
items.summary: OpenClaw is a local AI agent criticized for not being innovative enough compared to MS-DOS. It's seen as outdated by some users. The discussion highlights skepticism about its value.

items.title: GitHub's fake star economy
items.author: Liriel
items.summary: GitHub has been identified with a fake star economy involving six million suspected fake stars across thousands of repositories, primarily driven by bot accounts. No legal charges have been made yet. GitHub removed most flagged repositories but only half of the associated accounts.

items.title: Figma's woes compound with Claude Design
items.author: martinald
items.summary: Figma faces challenges as Anthropic's Claude Design enters the market, potentially drawing users away. Figma's userbase skews towards non-designers, and its AI design product lacks competitiveness. Anthropic's new tool could gain traction despite not fully replacing Figma's core offerings.

items.title: Show HN: Holos – QEMU/KVM with a compose-style YAML, GPUs and health checks
items.author: zeroecco
items.text:
I got tired of libvirt XML and Vagrant's Ruby/reload dance for single-host VM stacks, so I built a compose-style runtime directly on QEMU/KVM.

What's there: GPU passthrough as a first-class primitive (VFIO, OVMF, per-instance EFI vars), healthchecks that gate depends_on over SSH, socket-multicast L2 between VMs with no root and no bridge config, cloud-init wired through the YAML, Dockerfile support for provisioning.

What it's not: Kubernetes. No clustering, no live m
items.summary: Holos is a tool for QEMU/KVM virtualization using compose-style YAML, supporting GPUs and health checks. It simplifies setup and monitoring. GitHub repository is at github.com/zeroecco/holos.

items.title: Linux PTP mainline development war story and new features
items.author: ahlCVA
items.summary: The Linux PTP mainline development added support for selecting hardware timestamp sources, addressing a limitation, and was led by Russell King. Recent updates continue to enhance this functionality.

items.title: AI Resistance: some recent anti-AI stuff that’s worth discussing
items.author: speckx
items.summary: Recent anti-AI discussions focus on fears of capitalism, ownership, and control. Key points include resistance to AI's impact on jobs and privacy. Critics argue for regulation to protect human interests.
